content:textarea:3:60.name: Paragraph
content:textarea:3:60.html: When you support The Salvation Army of Oconee County, you're making a positive impact on lives of our most vulnerable neighbors. There are many ways you can help support our mission programs in our community.

content:wysiwyg_basic:4:5.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:4:5.html:
Donate Funds

To donate by phone, please call 1-800-SAL-ARMY (1-800-725-2769).

To text to give, text OconeeOnline to 41444.

Mail checks to: The Salvation Army, 100 Debra Street, Seneca, SC 29678.

To make a donation online, click the button below.

Donate

content:wysiwyg_basic:6:0.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:6:0.html:
Volunteer

We are always looking for committed, dedicated volunteers to help us Do the Most Good in Oconee County, SC.

If you're interested in learning more about volunteer opportunities with us, please contact Debra Andrews at 864-882-1160 or email Debra.Andrews@uss.salvationarmy.org.

content:wysiwyg_basic:8:35.name: WYSIWYG
content:wysiwyg_basic:8:35.html:
Red Kettle Campaign

All funds raised through our annual Red Kettle Campaign remain in Oconee County to help support our service programs throughout the year.

Learn More

design:columns:3 Columns:6:42.name: 3 Columns

content:wysiwyg_basic:11:77:0:16.html:
Angel Tree Program

Holidays often bring added stress to already difficult financial situations. The Salvation Army is here to lend a helping hand to families and vulnerable adults struggling during this time. We help provide Christmas gifts, coats and shoes to children ages 0 to 12 years of age and vulnerable adults. 

Click below to learn how you can help make the holidays a little more bright for these individuals.

Learn More

content:wysiwyg_basic:13:77:0:58.html:
Food Pantry donations

During times of crisis, The Salvation Army is here to lend a helping hand. The Salvation Army helps Oconee residents with emergency need of non-perishable grocery items. You can help donate non-perishable items to support our neighbors in need. For more information, call 864-882-1160.

Learn More

content:wysiwyg_basic:15:46:0:81.html:
Follow Us

Follow us on social media to stay up to date on the latest happenings with The Salvation Army of Oconee, and learn how you can get involved.

design:columns:1columninformational:6:68.name: 1 Column - Informational White

content:wysiwyg_basic:17:77:0:86.html:
Shop And Donate Goods

The Salvation Army Thrift Stores are frequented by so many different people – some looking for their own unique fashion sense, some looking for a deal, and some because they have nowhere else to go. Shopping and Donating are a great way to support our local community. When you donate goods to The Salvation Army, the items are then sold through our Family Stores, and the proceeds are used to fund life-saving programs in your community. It's a win-win!

The Salvation Army has four Thrift Store locations in the Upstate:

Seneca Thrift Store | 513 By Pass 123, Seneca, SC 29678 | 9 AM-5 PM (Mon-Sat) | (864) 882-7450

Clemson Thrift Store | 500B Old Greenville Highway, Clemson, 29631 | 9 AM-5:30 PM (Mon-Fri) & 9AM-5PM (Sat) |  (864) 722-5388

Greenville Thrift Store | 2402 E. North St,  Greenville 29609 | 9 AM-5:30 PM (Mon-Fri) & 9 AM-5 PM (Sat) | 864-233-1627

Taylors Thrift Store | 5152 Wade Hampton Boulevard, Taylors 29687 | 9 AM-5:30 PM (Mon-Fri) & 9AM-5PM (Sat) | (864) 534-1605

Donations may be made at any of the store locations during business hours. Commonly donated items include:  men's and women's clothing, children's clothing, furniture, household goods, electronics, and furniture in good condition. Tax-deductible receipts for your donation are available at the store locations. Save your receipt; donations to Salvation Army Thrift Stores are tax deductible!

 
Schedule a Pick Up

If you live in Oconee County and would like to schedule a pickup, call 864-233-1627.
